summary: Created repo agent instructions, learning notes scaffold, and canonical execution state for main checkout.
Configured local markdown issue tracker, default triage labels, and repo-specific single-context domain docs for engineering skills.
Mirrored the Agent skills setup into AGENTS.md so Codex reads the same issue tracker, triage, and domain-doc configuration.
Implemented context processor interface, no-op pipeline runner, feature-flagged chat hook, observability header/logging, timeout isolation, and regression tests.
Added policy-gated context pipeline activation task and set it as the next runnable .ai task.
Checked implementation evidence, marked ISSUE-001 through ISSUE-006 plus ISSUE-061 and ISSUE-062 done, and marked remaining planned issues ready for agent. Next canonical task set to ISSUE-007.
Added backlog triage state sync note for keeping 05-issues labels aligned with canonical .ai tasks and next-task selection.

Completed Sprint 02 registry and adapters: DB foundation, registry schema, immutable in-memory registry, internal profiles, OpenAI-compatible adapter, streaming skeleton, and fixed-point cost estimator. Next canonical task set to ISSUE-014.
Added immutable registry snapshot and streaming adapter compatibility notes for fast-path snapshot immutability and optional streaming provider seams.
Synkade .ai/tasks.json och state.json mot 05-issues labels: classifier/descriptor-spåret (014–019) markerat done, kanonisk next satt till ISSUE-020 (policy DSL v1).

Policy DSL v1 dokumenterad i 06-engineering/01-routing-policy-reference.md: top-level-schema, settings, when (13 villkor med AND), route (block/force/constraints/defaults + hint-bakåtkompat-mappning), prioritet och merge, full v1-vokabulär (task/risk/sensitivity/router_mode/profiles/capabilities), komplett default-policy och policytest-format. Alignment-pekare lagt i 01-architecture/07-policy-engine.md. Docs-only, ingen produktkod.

Policy parser och validering: internal/policy/{types,parser,parser_test}.go. Parse() validerar v1-schema (version/settings/rules), vokabulär (task/risk/sensitivity/router_mode/profiles/capabilities), unika rule-ids, well-formedness av when/route inkl. block/force/constraints/defaults och bakåtkompat-hints. Validate() cross-checkar providers/models mot registry.Snapshot. 17 tester, alla gröna. Lade till gopkg.in/yaml.v3-dep.

Implemented compiled policy cache in internal/policy: registry-validated CompiledPolicy snapshots, precomputed matchers, tenant/project/default Cache lookup, atomic reload that preserves last good policy on invalid candidates, route-hint normalization, and documented constraint merge semantics. Added focused compiled policy/cache tests plus learning note. Verification: go test ./internal/policy; go test ./...

Implemented policy explanations in internal/policy: Evaluation now returns matched rule ids, safe explanation fragments, first-match-wins force shadow explanations, structured log fields, and X-Router-Explain header activation helper. Added focused tests for matched-rule explanations, shadowed force fields, safe log fields, and header parsing. Verification: go test ./internal/policy; go test ./...; git diff --check.

Implemented offline policy test runner in internal/policy: YAML case parsing for single case, cases list, and top-level list formats; compiled policy execution against EvaluationInput; expected route assertions for matched rules, block state, model profile/name, verifier, required capabilities, and explanation fragments; CI-friendly report/error output. Runner tests also caught and fixed default merge semantics so specific defaults survive later catch-all defaults. Verification: go test ./internal/policy; go test ./...; jq empty .ai/state.json .ai/tasks.json; git diff --check.

Ran simplify cleanup over the policy runner/explanation diff. Replaced local slice helpers with standard-library slices helpers and simplified log-field test serialization without behavior changes. Verification: go test ./internal/policy; go test ./...; git diff --check.

Fixed code-review finding in policy test runner: YAML policy test cases now reject unknown suite/case/input/expected fields and reject empty expected assertions to prevent false passing tests. Added regression coverage and learning note. Verification: go test ./internal/policy.

Reconciled .ai state with main after Sprint 05-07 branches were merged. Marked ISSUE-025..041 done: routing engine + scoring + fallback + /router/decision dry-run + provider timeout/retry + streaming fallback (Sprint 05), async event queue + Prometheus metrics + spend aggregation + dashboard + health worker (Sprint 06), eval dataset/runner + outcome API + acceptance feedback + regression suite (Sprint 07). Code verified present in main across internal/engine, internal/eventlog, internal/metrics, internal/spend, internal/health, internal/evals, internal/outcomes, internal/server. Ledger had drifted because that work landed on branches without updating .ai/.

Fixed under-classification of prose-described coding/debugging prompts in internal/classifier. Added 'debug' keyword group (debug, stack trace, traceback, race condition, deadlock, panic, segfault, null pointer, exception phrases, memory leak, runtime/syntax error, crashing, ...) and 'code' group (concurrent, goroutine, mutex, thread-safe, '<lang> code', compile, unit test, ...) in features.go; both feed RequiresCode. hasDebugSignal now fires on the debug keyword (or a real stack trace) plus a code signal so 'debug this race condition deadlock in my concurrent Go code' classifies as hard_code_debugging instead of simple_chat/cheap-general. Code-bearing prompts now count as strong task intent (avoid unknown_high_risk). Reclassified the panic-fix case in router/job_test.go. Table tests added in features_test.go and task_test.go. Feature-extraction p95 stays ~12ms (<20ms budget). Verification: go test ./... ; merged to main (commit 81e8f1d).

Implemented secret masking v1. New pure, regex-based internal/secrets package masks API keys (sk-/sk-ant-/sk-proj-), AWS/Google/GitHub tokens, JWTs, Bearer tokens, DB/broker URIs with inline passwords, and generic secret-named key/value pairs (incl JSON), replacing values with [REDACTED:<kind>] while preserving non-secret context. Wired into the client-facing error boundary (writeError/writeSSEError in internal/server) so provider error strings carrying DB URLs/keys can never reach the client; emits a structured secret_masked event (count + types only, never the value) via the configured logger. Fast success path untouched - masking only runs on error responses, so routing latency budget is unaffected. Tests: internal/secrets/mask_test.go (table-driven: API keys, JWT, DB URLs, key=value, AWS/GitHub, multi-secret, benign no-op) and a server test proving secrets never reach the client and the masking event is logged without leaking. Verification: go test -race ./... all green; go vet clean.

Provider allow/deny per project: enforced allow/deny lists on the pinned-model paths so an override can never bypass a denylist. Provider/model allow+deny checks extracted from hardFilter into shared engine.providerModelConstraintReason (single source of truth). engine.decidePinned (explicit client model AND policy force.model) and decideDisabled (router_mode=disabled) now block (403, ErrBlocked, code provider_not_allowed) any pinned model that violates AllowedProviders/DeniedProviders/AllowedModels/DeniedModels, instead of honoring it as before. Policy already scoped per {TenantID,ProjectID} with deny unioned / allow intersected across layers. Tests added in engine_test.go: explicit client model denied by model list, by provider list, outside allowlist, allowed model honored, disabled-mode denied model blocked, and force.model cannot bypass deny. Verification: go test -race ./... all green; go vet clean.

Reconciled stale canonical .ai state with issue-file completion evidence on main. Marked ISSUE-044..052, ISSUE-056, and ISSUE-060 done in .ai/tasks.json because their issue files are state: done with implementation closeouts. Set .ai/state.json next to ISSUE-063, the remaining ready P1 task, and last_completed to ISSUE-060.

Implemented RBAC skeleton in auth/tenant/server: added explicit admin/user roles separate from scopes, introduced role middleware with legacy-unset role compatibility, and protected dashboard HTML/JSON routes as admin-only while leaving chat/decision/outcome scope behavior unchanged. Verification: go test ./internal/auth ./internal/server ./internal/tenant -race -count=1.
Added RBAC role compatibility note covering separate role vs scope checks, legacy unset-role behavior, and admin-only dashboard gating.

Implemented offline A/B policy simulation in internal/evals and cmd/eval-report. Added a shared engine.DecisionComparison contract for stable route/cost diffs, policy file compilation helpers, deterministic comparison aggregation, and comparison artifacts (comparison.json/comparison.txt). Eval cases can now carry tenant/project scope and metadata for policy simulation, and new fixtures cover premium-vs-balanced routing plus experiment-only blocking without any provider execution. Verification: go test ./internal/evals ./internal/policy ./internal/engine ./cmd/eval-report -count=1; go run ./cmd/eval-report -dataset evals/policy-sim-dataset.yaml -policy-a evals/policy-sim-a.yaml -policy-b evals/policy-sim-b.yaml.
Added decision comparison determinism note covering route-field-only diffs, micro-USD aggregation, and separating policy-version metadata from changed-decision counts.

Implemented model-specific prompt adapter skeleton in internal/provider and internal/server. The adapter is disabled by default, only mutates existing system-role messages on a cloned request, supports exact-model and profile-targeted prefix/suffix rules, preserves message order, and logs applied rule names without prompt content. Verification: go test ./internal/provider ./internal/server -race -count=1; git diff --check -- internal/provider internal/server.
Added system prompt adapter boundaries note covering clone-then-commit mutations, existing system-role message wrapping, and disabled-by-default adapter activation.

Implemented an offline cost-quality frontier section in internal/evals and cmd/eval-report. Regular eval reports now group candidate models by task class, estimate average per-task cost across the eval dataset, blend routed eval pass-rate with registry quality priors for frontier quality v1, mark Pareto-frontier vs dominated models, and emit deterministic best_value/lowest_cost/highest_quality recommendations with explicit empty/single/tie handling. Verification: go test ./internal/evals ./internal/spend ./cmd/eval-report -count=1.
Added cost-quality frontier determinism note covering eval-plus-prior smoothing, keeping outcomes separate from frontier scoring, and deterministic frontier recommendation tie-breaks.

Implemented opt-in shadow routing in internal/server and internal/eventlog. The chat handler can now compute an alternate policy decision with the shared engine without executing any shadow provider call, persist a shared DecisionComparison on decision events, and expose aggregate/recent actual-vs-shadow diffs through dashboard JSON/HTML while leaving admin-role dashboard protection intact. Verification: go test ./internal/server ./internal/eventlog ./internal/engine -count=1.

Updated the decision comparison determinism note with live shadow-routing guidance: persist the shared comparison payload on decision events, keep micro-USD aggregation stable, and ensure shadow evaluation never triggers a second provider execution.

Implemented offline trained lightweight classifier experiment in internal/classifier and internal/evals. Added deterministic experiment-only perceptron training/evaluation over a committed dataset, compared against current rule baseline, documented no production rollout without a future ADR, and left router.NewJobDescriptor production classification unchanged. Verification: go test ./internal/classifier ./internal/evals -count=1.

Implemented policy-gated context pipeline activation. Policy DSL now supports route.force.context_pipeline, runtime bootstrap wires a PolicyCache, context processors run after policy-backed routing and before non-streaming provider calls, the env flag is an operator rollout/kill switch, streaming remains skipped, and client headers/metadata cannot enable processors. Verification: go test ./internal/policy ./internal/server ./internal/contextproc -race -count=1; go test ./cmd/router -count=1.

Completed the Policy-Gated Routing Intelligence sprint via parallel subagents: policy-gated context pipeline, prompt adapter skeleton, offline policy A/B simulation, shadow routing, RBAC skeleton, offline lightweight classifier experiment, and cost-quality frontier reporting. Canonical next is null because no ready tasks remain in .ai/tasks.json pending a future triage pass.

Addressed final code-review findings for the sprint: cmd/router now loads runtime and shadow policy files, wires prompt adapter and comparison tracker into shipped server config/event fan-out, and cmd/eval-report persists real policy labels in comparison.txt. Added cmd/router package-main tests for policy file loading, prompt adapter activation, and shadow comparison fan-out. Verification: go test ./cmd/router ./cmd/eval-report -count=1.
Added runtime policy wiring operability note for policy-gated server features whose package tests pass via injected config but still need shipped binary wiring.
Added offline classifier experiment boundary note for keeping trained classifier experiments out of production routing until an ADR approves rollout.

Found a streaming fallback issue: first-token timeout advances to the next candidate without canceling the abandoned stream attempt, which can leak adapter goroutines or upstream response bodies. Updated the streaming adapter compatibility learning note and index with per-attempt cancellation guidance. Verification: go test ./...

Fixed streaming fallback cancellation. Each candidate now runs with a cancelable per-attempt context, pre-token fallback paths cancel abandoned streams, provider stream send loops respect context cancellation, and first-chunk errors are handled before first-token headers are written. Added regression coverage for canceling a timed-out candidate. Verification: go test ./internal/server -run TestStreamWithFallbackCancelsTimedOutCandidate -count=1; go test ./internal/provider -count=1; go test ./internal/server -count=1; go test ./...
